locked_decision: Use patch-level O/X only as a gate, not as the final task. Train count-free occupancy plus positive-patch spatial distribution; final density is sigmoid(occupancy_logit) multiplied by masked spatial softmax.
description: Long count-free gated-density run on the fixed 25pct day split. The scalar O/X head gates empty patches, while the spatial head learns the positive-patch distribution. No count regression head is used; final heatmap mass is occupancy probability, allocated by spatial probability.

patching.loss_weight: valid_crop only for O/X target construction; spatial loss is positive-patch normalized and optional sample terms use normalized log1p(anchor_lifetime)

partitioning.policy: PH anchors claim valid sea pixels first; fallback grid assigns every remaining valid sea pixel exactly once; halo pixels provide context only.

hierarchical_ph.policy: Large H0 parent anchors are not kept as oversized training patches. Local H0 PH is rerun inside each large parent with relaxed child detection; child anchors claim local peaks first and the existing fallback grid covers any remainder.

loss.name: occupancy_spatial_loss
loss.rationale: Patch-level O/X is used as a gate for whether the spatial distribution should contribute mass. Spatial supervision remains positive-patch normalized. The run uses lower LR, dropout, and longer training; checkpoint selection should compare best_val_occupancy_f1 and best_val_spatial_overlap.
